title: Lathyrus platyphyllos
taxonomy: Lathyrus platyphyllos is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Lathyrus, which includes annual and perennial species. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean. Within the Fabaceae family, Lathyrus platyphyllos is part of the subfamily Faboideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

side_effects: Lathyrus platyphyllos is known to cause neurolathyrism, a condition that affects the nervous system, when ingested in large quantities over a prolonged period. The disease is characterized by muscle weakness, paralysis, and in severe cases, can lead to permanent damage to the nervous system. Consuming large amounts of Lathyrus platyphyllos can also cause damage to the spinal cord, resulting in loss of sensation and movement. The condition is often linked to food shortages, where people are forced to eat the plant as a last resort. intro: Lathyrus platyphyllos, also known as the broad-leaved pea or broad-leaved evergreen pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus platyphyllos include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its health benefits.
